symptom: a lifelong limp and several old leg fractures
hook: the top of the thighbone had curved into the same hook shape as a shepherd's staff
whatYouSee: The top of the thighbone curved sharply downward into the same hook shape as a shepherd's staff.
whyItMatters: Normal bone in that area was slowly replaced by weaker fibrous tissue so the bone bent under the body's own weight over years.
treatment: Surgeons straighten the bone with a cut and an internal rod and bisphosphonates can ease the bone pain.
takeaway: A thighbone can bend itself into the shape of a walking cane.
igOptions: C. Shepherd's crook deformity from fibrous dysplasia

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a lifelong limp and several old leg fractures.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the top of the thighbone had curved into the same hook shape as a shepherd's staff.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Shepherd's crook deformity (fibrous dysplasia)

👀 What you see:
The top of the thighbone curved sharply downward into the same hook shape as a shepherd's staff.

🦴 Why it matters:
Normal bone in that area was slowly replaced by weaker fibrous tissue so the bone bent under the body's own weight over years.

💊 Treatment:
Surgeons straighten the bone with a cut and an internal rod and bisphosphonates can ease the bone pain.

seedHint: Look at the very top of the thighbone and ask yourself if it curves left or right.
